figures.title: Overall Framework of Unified Dual-Mask Physical Model
figures.caption: The overall framework of our proposed method. The light field signal is first decoupled via Three-Layer Angular Signal Decomposition to extract physical priors. These priors guide the GeometricDualMask architecture to adaptively route Lambertian and non-Lambertian regions through specialized branches, followed by a pixel-wise dual-mask fusion for robust depth estimation.
annotations: Key insight: Three-layer decomposition replaces traditional frequency-domain analysis.
Dual-branch design explicitly handles X-shaped EPI patterns in non-Lambertian regions.

figures.title: Three-Layer Angular Signal Decomposition
figures.caption: Detailed design of the physical signal decomposition module. The input EPI signal is decoupled into ambient illumination, parallax-induced depth, and material-specific residual components. The material residual is utilized to compute angular gradient pseudo-labels for robust material classification.
annotations: Avoids frequency-domain limitations of low angular resolution commercial cameras.

figures.title: GeometricDualMask Architecture
figures.caption: The dual-branch adaptive routing architecture. The EPI branch with Plane Regularized Sampling Operator (PRSO) processes diffuse Lambertian surfaces, while the Geometric Convolution branch leverages X-shaped patterns for reflective non-Lambertian surfaces. A pixel-wise mask seamlessly integrates the outputs.
annotations: PRSO enforces plane regularization for diffuse regions.
Geometric Conv captures bimodal distributions of X-shaped EPI patterns.

figures.title: Qualitative Comparison on Mixed Scenes
figures.caption: Visual comparison of depth estimation results on mixed scenes containing both Lambertian and non-Lambertian materials. Our method successfully recovers depth in specular and glossy regions where baseline EPI methods suffer from severe structural discontinuities.
annotations: Red boxes highlight non-Lambertian regions with X-shaped EPI patterns.
Our method significantly reduces depth ambiguity in reflective surfaces.

figures.title: Ablation Study of Core Components
figures.caption: Ablation study demonstrating the contribution of each proposed module. The unified multi-domain balanced training framework and the dual-branch architecture provide the most significant performance gains, especially in non-Lambertian regions.
annotations: Balanced training mitigates the severe data scarcity bottleneck of non-Lambertian scenes.

issues.description: The grouping boxes (dashed and solid) have similar visual weight, making it slightly unclear which group contains which modules. The dashed box for 'Physical Prior Extraction' and solid box for 'Adaptive Routing & Fusion' could be more distinct to improve hierarchy.
issues.suggestion: Use a more distinct style for the grouping boxes, such as different line styles (dashed vs. solid) with varying opacity or colors, and ensure the boxes properly enclose all sub-nodes without overlapping. For example, make the 'Physical Prior Extraction' box dashed with a lighter blue and the 'Adaptive Routing & Fusion' box solid with a slightly darker blue to enhance visual separation.

report.summary: A well-structured framework diagram with clear data flow and prominent innovation modules, though grouping boxes could be more visually distinct.

issues.description: Annotation text 'Avoids frequency-domain limitations of low angular resolution commercial cameras.' is partially cut off at the bottom.
issues.suggestion: Extend the figure height or reduce font size to ensure full text visibility.

report.summary: The figure effectively visualizes the three-layer angular signal decomposition with clear data flow and prominent innovation modules, though minor text truncation exists.

issues.description: Pixel-wise Mask module lacks distinct visual differentiation from other innovation modules, reducing emphasis on its role in fusion.
issues.suggestion: Apply a unique fill color or border style (e.g., dashed border) to the Pixel-wise Mask node to visually distinguish it while maintaining harmony with the blue innovation theme.

report.summary: The figure effectively communicates the dual-branch architecture with clear data flow and proper module grouping, though the Pixel-wise Mask could be more visually distinct.

issues.suggestion: Replace the placeholder with a diagram containing all expected modules (Baseline Model, With Decomposition, With Dual-Branch, With Balanced Training, Full Model) and group them as 'Progressive Additions' to demonstrate component contributions.
